summary: Joe Reis shares observations from his international conference tour, noting that while GenAI enthusiasm remains high, actual production implementations are rare, and practitioners are growing cynical about the gap between demos and reality. He observes that Databricks is gaining momentum over Snowflake in practitioner conversations, data teams continue to be undervalued as cost centers, and the 2020/21 data vendor landscape is consolidating to clear winners.
key_points: GenAI moved from imagining use cases (2023) to POC phase (2024), but production implementations remain extremely rare
The AI hype cycle appears to be past its peak, with practitioners tired of noise and demanding real results
Data vendor landscape from 2020/21 is consolidating - top 3 in each category are emerging, others face difficult futures
Databricks is gaining momentum in practitioner conversations while Snowflake is increasingly seen as lagging
Open table formats like Iceberg generate buzz but have few production implementations yet
money_quotes: The gap between enthusiasm and real-world implementation is massive.
I'm long-term bullish on AI, but I feel this hype cycle is played out by a crowd of grifters and sameness. Show us the money!
Data's still a mess. Most data initiatives fail. Data teams are seen as a cost center and not getting the support they deserve. Same as it ever was.
Big tech doesn't apply. We're just talking plain Jane data stacks and mostly conventional use cases.
key_insight: Despite massive AI hype, the data industry's fundamental challenges remain unchanged—most initiatives fail, teams are undervalued, and the gap between vendor promises and production reality continues to widen.
